---
title: General Insights
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
df <- read.csv("ResaleflatpricesbasedonregistrationdatefromJan2017onwards.csv")
head(df)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

#calculate the five-number summary for the box plot (excluding outliers)
bp_stats <- boxplot.stats(df$floor_area_sqm)$stats

#box plot
ggplot(df, aes(x = floor_area_sqm, y = "")) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", color = "darkblue", outlier.color = "red", outlier.size = 2) + 
  
  #text labels for the statistics
  geom_text(data = data.frame(x = bp_stats, y = ""), 
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = x), 
            vjust = -2, size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Floor Area in HDB Resale Flats",
    subtitle = "Numbers represent the min, 25th percentile, median, 75th percentile, and max (excluding outliers)",
    x = "Floor Area (Square Meters)",
    y = ""
  ) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(), 
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

#calculate the five-number summary for the box plot (excluding outliers)
bp_stats_price <- boxplot.stats(df$resale_price)$stats

#box plot
ggplot(df, aes(x = resale_price, y = "")) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "forestgreen", color = "darkgreen", outlier.color = "red", outlier.size = 2) + 
  
  #text labels and format them as currency so they are readable
  geom_text(data = data.frame(x = bp_stats_price, y = ""), 
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = dollar(x)), 
            vjust = -2, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
            
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of HDB Resale Prices",
    subtitle = "Numbers represent the min, 25th percentile, median, 75th percentile, and max",
    x = "Resale Price (SGD)",
    y = ""
  ) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(), 
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
#label encode flat type, storey_range, flat_model
#town
town_mapping <- levels(factor(df$town))
df$town <- as.integer(factor(df$town))

#flat_type
flat_type_mapping <- levels(factor(df$flat_type))
df$flat_type <- as.integer(factor(df$flat_type))

#storey_range
storey_range_mapping <- levels(factor(df$storey_range))
df$storey_range <- as.integer(factor(df$storey_range))

#flat model
flat_model_mapping <- levels(factor(df$flat_model))
df$flat_model <- as.integer(factor(df$flat_model))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

#convert text to date format
df$month_date <- ym(df$month) 

#summary table
storey_year_avg <- df %>%
  mutate(year = year(month_date)) %>%
  group_by(year, storey_range) %>%
  summarise(avg_price = mean(resale_price, na.rm = TRUE),.groups = 'drop')

#convert storey_range back to labels for the plot
storey_year_avg$storey_range <- factor(storey_year_avg$storey_range, 
                                       levels = 1:length(storey_range_mapping), 
                                       labels = storey_range_mapping)
```


```{r}
#average resale price trend

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

#list of all the unique storey ranges
unique_storeys <- unique(storey_year_avg$storey_range)

#we loop through each storey range and create an individual plot
individual_plots <- lapply(unique_storeys, function(current_storey) {
  
  #filter dataset for just this specific storey range
  plot_data <- storey_year_avg %>% filter(storey_range == current_storey)
  
  #line graph
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = year, y = avg_price)) +                    
    geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +                                                                
    geom_point(color = "darkblue", size = 2) +
    
    #price labels per point (formatted as $XXXk)
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0("$", round(avg_price/1000, 0), "k")), 
              vjust = -1.2, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
    
    #vertical space so the text doesn't get cut off at the top
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +                                                                      
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(plot_data$year), max(plot_data$year), by = 1)) +
    
    labs(          
      #able to dynamoically set the title so we know which storey range we are looking at
      title = paste("Average Resale Price Trend - Storey Range:", current_storey),                                
      x     = "Year",                                                                        
      y     = "Average Resale Price (SGD)"
    ) +                                                                                      
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
    )
  
  return(p)
})

#1,2,3,4 : we focus from floors 1-12
print(individual_plots[[4]])
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

#summary table for Year-on-Year data
flat_year_avg <- df %>%
  mutate(year = year(ym(month))) %>%
  group_by(year, flat_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_price = mean(resale_price, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  #map labels back to the flat_type integers
  mutate(flat_type_label = flat_type_mapping[flat_type])

ggplot(flat_year_avg, aes(x = factor(year), y = avg_price, group = flat_type_label, color = flat_type_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  #to show numbers on chart
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("$", round(avg_price/1000, 0), "k")), 
            vjust = -1, size = 3, fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.2))) +
  labs(
    title = "Year-on-Year Average Resale Price by Flat Type",
    subtitle = "Prices labeled in thousands (k)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Average Resale Price (SGD)",
    color = "Flat Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
